title: Atalantia spinosa
intro: Atalantia spinosa, also known as the Chinese atalantia or Chinese bitter orange, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to China and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to its ability to aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of A. spinosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. A. spinosa is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional Chinese medicine formulas and supplements for its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Atalantia spinosa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is a member of the genus Atalantia, which belongs to the order Sapindales. The family Rutaceae, also known as the citrus or rue family, consists of trees and shrubs that are commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Atalantia spinosa is native to Southeast Asia and is often found growing in woodland areas and along streams. The species is considered to be a close relative of other plants in the Rutaceae family, such as citrus and rue species.
